text: Code reviews are a key part of software development. In a code review, one developer checks another developer’s code before it is added to the main project. This process is very helpful for catching bugs early. When a small mistake is found and fixed quickly, it saves time and prevents bigger problems later. Code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing. Team members learn different techniques and understand parts of the project they did not write themselves. This helps everyone grow their skills. Finally, code reviews improve team quality. The team develops a shared standard for writing code, which makes the final product more reliable and easier to maintain.
